title: Mission charter (Δ-18)
description: Δ-18 charter pre-authorization for an orchestrator run. T0 base + at least one T1 profile overlay. Hard validator rules (Constitution §1.7-C/§1.7-D + process/delivery-loop.md §4.2.2): MANDATORY_CHECKPOINTS cannot be bypassed in any of four shapes (omitted/emptied/disabled/overridden); tooling.acceptance.on_fix_required.human_confirm_required MUST be true; tooling.acceptance.on_fix_required.route_options MUST be non-empty.

goal.description: One-line user-facing goal Customer reads at gate 2.

requirements.description: Δ-19 (design §5.2). OPTIONAL requirement-ledger wiring. Absent ⇒ default path; the ledger is still optional (Phase 2-alpha is additive).

ledger_path.description: Path to the project requirement ledger (requirement-ledger.schema.json).

required_framework_capabilities.description: Phase-4 native-E2E capability contract (design §2/§13). OPTIONAL, ADDITIVE. The machine-readable framework capabilities this charter REQUIRES the deployed/pinned aidazi to PROVIDE — checked DETERMINISTICALLY + FAIL-CLOSED at plan sign-off (--sign-plan) and at the real-run preflight against the framework-owned governance/framework-capabilities.json contract. A missing/under-versioned capability REFUSES the sign/run and names the missing capability, the deployed framework capability/version, and the upgrade action. Absent ⇒ no pinned requirement (legacy-safe, byte-identical to pre-Phase-4). Lives in the charter, so it is bound into charter_hash ⊂ the signed campaign hash H — a post-sign change is an authority change → re-sign.

id.description: Capability id declared by governance/framework-capabilities.json, e.g. native_managed_external_e2e | framework_owned_e2e_provenance | autonomous_e2e_remediation | codex_adapter_liveness.

min_version.description: Minimum required capability version (dotted integers, compared component-wise). Absent ⇒ presence of the capability suffices.

task_signals.description: OPTIONAL mission-level signal profile from the CLOSED task-signal vocabulary (single source of truth: effective_role_config.TASK_SIGNAL_VOCAB; drift-guarded). Universal-skill-mounting design (archive/2026-07-06): governs task-aware skill selection for spawns with NO sub-sprint plan entry (research, deliver-decompose, all delivery_only spawns); a sub-sprint's own signed task_signals govern EXCLUSIVELY when a plan entry exists (most-specific-wins); Acceptance is always excluded (§3.6 calibration). Human-signed by construction: the charter is the adopter trust root, and in campaign mode charter_hash sits inside signed_scope_H. Absent ⇒ byte-identical behavior.

e2e_remediation.description: P3 §1.7-G (design §5.3). User-authored, SIGNED budget for the autonomous browser_e2e remediation lane: on a DETERMINISTIC criterion-bound executor failure (checklist-results.json executor_status ∈ {fail,error} from a FULL managed real-execution run) the orchestrator may, WITHOUT a fresh human-confirm checkpoint, dispatch a bounded in-envelope Dev fix + rerun + re-judge — NEVER shipping (checkpoint #9 is unchanged). Default-OFF: absent or enabled:false ⇒ deterministic criterion failures route to the existing §3.5 human gate exactly as today (legacy-safe). Enabled ONLY at autonomy.level human_on_the_loop or higher. This block is inside charter_hash ⊂ the campaign signed hash H, so raising any bound post-sign flips H → stale → re-sign (an authority change); a NORMAL remediation round adds no re-sign. The interpretive Acceptance fix_required / needs_human path stays §3.5 human-gated (§1.7-G is facts-only).

max_rounds.description: Max bounded fix→rerun→re-judge cycles before fail-closed HALT (driver._check_budget sibling cap on state.e2e_remediation_round). Required when enabled.

max_no_progress_rounds.description: Halt-threshold for a round whose failing-criterion set is NOT a strict proper subset of the prior round's (mirrors gap_followup). A round that INTRODUCES a new failing criterion is an immediate regression HALT. Default (absent) 1 — no-progress HALTs on the first occurrence; a value > 1 tolerates non-progress and is rejected by charter_validator as a §1.7-D evasion.

max_api_usd.description: Caveat: doesn't track subscription-billed agents (e.g., Codex).

tools.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet C, plan §5). OPTIONAL built-in tool allowlist in object form {allow:[...]}. Default-deny beyond the listed built-ins.
Built-in tool allowlist. Legacy array form (array of Read|Grep|Glob) OR v2 object form {allow:[...]} (plan §5). Read-only reviewer whitelist either way.

e2e.description: P-C. Browser-E2E executor MECHANICS (the HOW), conforming to schemas/executor-contract.schema.json. The charter validator cross-validates this against that schema; the driver RE-validates at the e2e gate (fail-closed, since run_loop validates only on allow_real). OPTIONAL; required when any milestone runs tooling.acceptance.functional.mode=browser_e2e. archive/2026-06-20-pc-browser-e2e-design.md §4.2.

mode.description: P-A. Acceptance run mode: off (no acceptance) | advisory (runs; a pass NEVER auto-ships — human signs off at the advisory_acceptance_pass_signoff checkpoint) | auto (runs; a pass auto-ships ONLY when authoritative = judge calibrated AND autonomy fully_autonomous_within_budget; otherwise advisory). New charters set this; absent ⇒ off. archive/2026-06-20-autonomous-delivery-design.md §3.1/§3.2.

enabled.description: DEPRECATED alias for `mode` (true→auto, false→off), normalized before validation by charter_compat. Optional; prefer `mode`. If both are present they MUST agree, else the validator errors.

harness.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Execution harness. OPTIONAL; judgment roles suit headless/API-backed models.

provider.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Provider id. OPTIONAL. Calibration is per-(role,provider,model) — a change invalidates judge_calibration.status (Constitution §3.6).

endpoint.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Base URL for OpenAI-compatible providers. OPTIONAL.

api_key_env.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). NAME of the env var holding the provider API key (value read at call time, never stored; keep it in a gitignored .env.local). OPTIONAL (headless judge).

endpoint_env.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). NAME of the env var holding the base URL when not given literally in `endpoint` (literal wins if both set). OPTIONAL.

capability_ref.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Capability-profile id validated against schemas/model-registry.schema.json; judgment roles require a calibratable model. OPTIONAL.

timeout_seconds.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Per-spawn wall-clock timeout (seconds) for the Acceptance adapter, read by run_loop.build_adapters on real runs. OPTIONAL; absent ⇒ the adapter default (600s).

connectors.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet C). Connector grants for Acceptance — default-deny; read-only evidence connectors only (judgment is not delegated).

discovery.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet C). OPTIONAL; default off.

sandbox.description: v2 ADDITIVE. Acceptance is a READ-ONLY judge; a CLI-backed harness (codex/claude_code) honors this. OPTIONAL.

network_access.description: v2 ADDITIVE. Explicit per-role network grant. The shipped role defaults set this true for all five LLM roles; explicit false disables it. For codex, the OS-sandbox network toggle is effective only on a workspace_write sandbox; on read_only roles it is a declared grant but remains a sandbox no-op.

subagent_fanout.description: OPTIONAL. Fan-out is discouraged for Acceptance; if true, sub-agents are read-only evidence-gatherers (Constitution §3.4 invariant #6).

record_path.description: Calibration record bound to the effective judge identity. New adopters should provide this before declaring calibrated.

functional.description: Browser-functional Acceptance. hybrid is the default interaction posture for new adopters: deterministic signed journeys plus Acceptance-authored exploratory journeys. Repository access remains read-only while the application/environment may be operated according to the explicit policy, including production when selected.

mode.description: static (M1; default) | browser_e2e (M3; runs the browser evidence gate). No schema default — absence ⇒ static at the driver.

interaction_mode.description: deterministic=fixed journeys only; agentic=Acceptance-authored journeys; hybrid=both (recommended default).

target_environment.description: Environment Acceptance is authorized to operate. production is permitted only with explicit origins and side-effect policy.

executor_contract.description: OPTIONAL id/path label for the executor-contract MECHANICS; the live mechanics are tooling.e2e (schemas/executor-contract.schema.json).

checklist_path.description: Path to the signed functional-checklist (schemas/functional-checklist.schema.json) — Research-owned CRITERIA, frozen at Gate-1. Required when mode=browser_e2e.

judge_calibration_m3.description: M3 (browser-functional) calibration, SEPARATE from the M1 judge_calibration above. Absent ⇒ uncalibrated ⇒ M3 advisory (v1 ships no M3 record).

record_path.description: Path to the browser_e2e acceptance-calibration-record JSON.

human_confirm_required.description: MUST be true per Constitution §1.7-C; charter validator rejects false.

route_options.description: MAY be narrowed; MAY NOT be empty.

profile_type_a.description: Type A overlay; populate if Type A or A+B hybrid.

profile_type_b.description: Type B overlay (PLACEHOLDER per OQ-V4-001 — full spec deferred until hermes first SOP milestone).

profile_type_c.description: Type C overlay; populate if Type C demo.

mandatory_checkpoints_added.description: Adopter-added custom MANDATORY_CHECKPOINTS (in addition to the 9 defaults). The 9 defaults cannot be omitted/emptied/disabled/overridden (Constitution §1.7-D; process/delivery-loop.md §4.2.2).

isolation.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Loop Ingress, plan §4.3/§5). Per-loop isolation strategy prompted at a new-loop trigger. OPTIONAL; this is a configurable ingress prompt, NOT one of the 9 MANDATORY_CHECKPOINTS. Existing charters omit it and validate unchanged.

prompt_on_new_loop.description: Prompt the human for isolation strategy at a new-loop trigger (continuations don't re-prompt).

default_strategy.description: current_branch = in-place (default). new_branch = discrete PR unit. new_worktree = parallel / long autonomous / fan-out.

worktree_root.description: Directory under which new worktrees are created (new_worktree strategy).

cleanup_policy.description: Disposition of an isolated branch/worktree at loop close.

force_isolation_when.description: Conditions under which the engine overrides toward isolation (recommend, with stated reason).

intent_contract.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Next2.1, plan §4.6/§5). Goal/standard/proof-of-done triple captured at ingress, anchoring loop_id (mirrors schemas/intent-contract.schema.json). OPTIONAL at charter level. Per §1.7-D/OQ-B, confirmed_by_human flips true ONLY by the authorized human; the engine never auto-confirms.

audit.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Audit Spine, plan §4.5/§5). Per-loop append-only hash-chained ledger config. OPTIONAL; audit is runtime-owned (Constitution §1.4). Events match schemas/audit-event.schema.json.

hash_chain.description: Tamper-evident hash chaining (each event commits to prev_hash).

memory.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Loop Memory substrate; modules/m-memory.md). OPTIONAL, runtime-owned md files (sibling of `audit`, Constitution §1.4). DEFAULT OFF: absent OR enabled:false ⇒ the driver never touches the store (byte-identical to no memory). When enabled, the driver injects prior generalizable lessons into role prompts at ingress and records lessons at close; it does NOT raise autonomy, does NOT auto-edit any load-bearing artifact, and adds NO checkpoint — but it DOES change prompt context, so the enable decision is recorded (adoption-state) and stays subject to the existing calibration policy (Constitution §3.6). CLI --memory-root overrides this block. NOTE: the `default` values below are documentation; the runtime resolver (engine-kit/scheduling/run_loop._resolve_memory_root) assigns them EXPLICITLY (JSON-Schema defaults are not runtime assignments).

enabled.description: Activate Loop Memory. Absent ⇒ treated as false ⇒ OFF. Only literal boolean true enables; the resolver defaults this to false explicitly.

root.description: Loop Memory root directory. A RELATIVE path resolves against the charter's directory and MUST stay contained within it — a `..`/absolute escape is rejected (a declarative root must not write outside the adopter tree; use --memory-root for an external path). Defaults to "memory" when memory is enabled without an explicit (non-empty) root.

harness.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A, plan §4.1/§5). Execution harness, decoupled from provider/model: claude_code | codex | headless | <other>. headless = OpenAI-compatible endpoint adapter (unlocks deepseek/moonshot/openai for non-editing roles). OPTIONAL; legacy charters use only agent_kind.

provider.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Provider id: anthropic | openai | deepseek | moonshot | <other>. OPTIONAL.

endpoint.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Base URL for OpenAI-compatible providers driven via the headless harness. OPTIONAL.

api_key_env.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). NAME of the env var holding the provider API key (e.g. DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) for the headless harness. The VALUE is read from the environment at call time and NEVER stored in the charter — keep it in a gitignored .env.local (loaded by run_loop on real runs). OPTIONAL.

endpoint_env.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). NAME of the env var holding the base URL when not given literally in `endpoint`. The literal `endpoint` wins if both are set. OPTIONAL.

capability_ref.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Capability-profile id validated against schemas/model-registry.schema.json (process/model-capability-registry.md). The capability gate checks the (harness, provider, model) triple against the role's requirements. OPTIONAL.

reasoning_effort.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Per-spawn reasoning effort for the bound harness: Claude Code maps to --effort; Codex maps to -c model_reasoning_effort=<value>. OPTIONAL; absent ⇒ harness default.

timeout_seconds.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet A). Per-spawn wall-clock timeout (seconds) for this role's adapter subprocess/HTTP call, read by run_loop.build_adapters on real runs. OPTIONAL; absent ⇒ the adapter default (600s). A real Dev coding session typically needs more (e.g. 1800–3600).

subagent_fanout.description: OPTIONAL. false forbids intra-role sub-agent fan-out for this role; true permits it (Constitution §3.4 invariant #6 constraints apply regardless). Omitted = backing agent's default behavior.

connectors.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet C, plan §4.1/§5). Per-role connector grants — DEFAULT-DENY: a role may use only the connectors listed here (distinct from built-in `tools`). OPTIONAL; omit ⇒ no connectors. See schemas/connector-binding.schema.json + connectors/registry.yaml.

discovery.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet C). Connector/tool discovery posture. OPTIONAL; default off (propose_only never auto-authorizes — scan is an authoring aid).

network_access.description: v2 ADDITIVE. Explicit per-role network grant. The shipped role defaults set this true for all five LLM roles; explicit false disables it. For codex, the OS-sandbox network toggle is effective only on a workspace_write sandbox; on read_only roles it is a declared grant but remains a sandbox no-op. The driver records a `sandbox_network_granted` audit event when the grant is present.

connector_binding.description: v2 ADDITIVE. A per-role connector grant; mirror of schemas/connector-binding.schema.json. Default-deny; secrets by-name only; scopes ⊆ role sandbox; server pinned.

server.description: Pinned '<ref>@<pin>' for mcp, base URL for http_api, command for cli. Unpinned/runtime-fetched is forbidden (Constitution §1.7 new item).

tools.description: OPTIONAL allowlist of connector tool/function names; omit ⇒ all (still bounded by scopes + sandbox).

secrets.description: Env-var NAMES only; values stay in the adopter env.

mode.description: off = no discovery. propose_only = scanner suggests connectors/tools for a human to approve into the allowlist; it NEVER auto-authorizes (default-deny is preserved).

tools_allow.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet C). Built-in tool allowlist, object form (plan §5 tooling.<role>.tools.allow[]).

allow.description: Built-in tools this role may use. Default-deny: tools not listed are not granted.

skill_binding.description: v2 ADDITIVE (Facet B). A per-role skill binding with explicit provenance; mirror of schemas/skill-binding.schema.json. Vendored + pinned; NO runtime fetch; tool_requirements ⊆ role whitelist; redistributable license.

pin.description: Immutable upstream commit SHA (provenance; no runtime fetch).

optional.description: Track 1 §2.2 — skip-if-absent: true ⇒ an unresolvable binding is skipped (skipped_skills[] + audit footer) instead of hard-failing; absent/false = required (mirror of schemas/skill-binding.schema.json).

skills_config.description: Omit to inherit framework role_defaults. Legacy array form replaces defaults. Object form supports inherit/extend/replace/disable explicitly.
